info.title: Adafruit IO REST API

get.summary: Get information about the current user
Get the user's data rate limit and current activity level.

200.description: Data rate limit and current actions.

data_rate_limit.description: Max possible actions inside the time window (usually 1 minute).

active_data_rate.description: Actions taken inside the time window.

data_rate_limit.description: Max possible actions inside the time window (usually 1 minute).

active_data_rate.description: Actions taken inside the time window.

get.description: The Activities endpoint returns information about the user's activities.

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: Start time for filtering, returns records created after given time.

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: End time for filtering, returns records created before give time.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit the number of records returned.

X-Pagination-Limit.description: The limit this request is using, either your given value or the default (1000).

X-Pagination-Start.description: The created_at value for the oldest record returned.

X-Pagination-End.description: The created_at value for the newest record returned.

get.description: The Feeds endpoint returns information about the user's feeds. The response includes the latest value of each feed, and other metadata about each feed.
Returns feed based on the feed key

get.summary: Get all data for the given feed

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: Start time for filtering, returns records created after given time.

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: End time for filtering, returns records created before give time.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit the number of records returned.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: List of Data record fields to include in response as comma separated list. Acceptable values are: `value`, `lat`, `lon`, `ele`, `id`, and `created_at`.

X-Pagination-Limit.description: The limit this request is using, either your given value or the default (1000).

X-Pagination-Start.description: The created_at value for the oldest record returned.

X-Pagination-End.description: The created_at value for the newest record returned.

post.description:
Create new data records on the given feed.

**NOTE:** when feed history is on, data `value` size is limited to 1KB, when feed history is turned off data value size is limited to 100KB.

requestBody.description: Data record including a `value` field (required) and optionally including: `lat`, `lon`, `ele` (latitude, longitude, and elevation values), and `created_at` (a date/time string).

x-code-samples.source:
api = Adafruit::IO::Client.new(
  key: ENV['IO_KEY'],
  username: ENV['IO_USERNAME']
)
api.send_data('io-feed-key', 65.5)

get.description:
The Chart API is what we use on io.adafruit.com to populate charts over varying timespans with a consistent number of data points. The maximum number of points returned is 480. This API works by aggregating slices of time into a single value by averaging.

All time-based parameters are optional, if none are given it will default to 1 hour at the finest-grained resolution possible.

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: Start time for filtering, returns records created after given time.

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: End time for filtering, returns records created before give time.

parameters.name: resolution
parameters.description: A resolution size in minutes. By giving a resolution value you will get back grouped data points aggregated over resolution-sized intervals. NOTE: time span is preferred over resolution, so if you request a span of time that includes more than max limit points you may get a larger resolution than you requested. Valid resolutions are 1, 5, 10, 30, 60, and 120.

parameters.name: hours
parameters.description: The number of hours the chart should cover.

200.description: A JSON record containing chart data and the parameters used to generate it.

columns.description: The names of the columns returned as data.
The names of the columns returned as data.

requestBody.description: A collection of data records including `value` (required) and optionally including: `lat`, `lon`, `ele` (latitude, longitude, and elevation values), and `created_at` (a date/time string).

get.description: Get the previously processed data point in the feed. NOTE: this method doesn't move the processing queue pointer.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: List of Data record fields to include in response as comma separated list. Acceptable values are: `value`, `lat`, `lon`, `ele`, `id`, and `created_at`.

get.description: Get the next newest data point in the feed. If queue processing hasn't been started, the first data point in the feed will be returned.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: List of Data record fields to include in response as comma separated list. Acceptable values are: `value`, `lat`, `lon`, `ele`, `id`, and `created_at`.

get.description: Get the most recent data point in the feed. This request sets the queue pointer to the end of the feed.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: List of Data record fields to include in response as comma separated list. Acceptable values are: `value`, `lat`, `lon`, `ele`, `id`, and `created_at`.

get.description: Get the oldest data point in the feed. This request sets the queue pointer to the beginning of the feed.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: List of Data record fields to include in response as comma separated list. Acceptable values are: `value`, `lat`, `lon`, `ele`, `id`, and `created_at`.

get.summary: Last Data in MQTT CSV format
get.description: Get the most recent data point in the feed in an MQTT compatible CSV format: `value,lat,lon,ele`

200.description: CSV string in `value,lat,lon,ele` format. The lat, lon, and ele values are left blank if they are not set.

get.description: The Groups endpoint returns information about the user's groups. The response includes the latest value of each feed in the group, and other metadata about the group.

patch.summary: Update properties of an existing Group

post.summary: Add an existing Feed to a Group
Remove a Feed from a Group

get.summary: All feeds for current user in a given group
get.description: The Group Feeds endpoint returns information about the user's feeds. The response includes the latest value of each feed, and other metadata about each feed, but only for feeds within the given group.

post.summary: Create a new Feed in a Group
Create new data for multiple feeds in a group

location.description: A location record with `lat`, `lon`, and [optional] `ele` properties.

feeds.description: An array of feed data records with `key` and `value` properties.

created_at.description: Optional created_at timestamp which will be applied to all feed values created.

feeds.description: An array of feed data records with `key` and `value` properties.

created_at.description: Optional created_at timestamp which will be applied to all feed values created.

get.summary: All data for current feed in a specific group

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: Start time for filtering data. Returns data created after given time.

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: End time for filtering data. Returns data created before give time.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit the number of records returned.

post.summary: Create new Data in a feed belonging to a particular group

requestBody.description: Data record including a `value` field (required) and optionally including: `lat`, `lon`, `ele` (latitude, longitude, and elevation values), and `created_at` (a date/time string).

post.summary: Create multiple new Data records in a feed belonging to a particular group

requestBody.description: A collection of data records including `value` (required) and optionally including: `lat`, `lon`, `ele` (latitude, longitude, and elevation values), and `created_at` (a date/time string).

post.summary: Send data to a feed via webhook URL.
Send arbitrary data to a feed via webhook URL.
post.description: The raw data webhook receiver accepts POST requests and stores the raw request body on your feed. This is useful when you don't have control of the webhook sender. If feed history is turned on, payloads will be truncated at 1024 bytes. If feed history is turned off, payloads will be truncated at 100KB.

shared_with.description: Access control list for this feed

count.description: Number of data points stored by this feed.

HeaderKey.description: The AIO Key is used to restrict or grant access to your data. The key is unique, and you can generate a key per feed, and control it in many different ways. The easiest process is to just use your automatically generated master key. You can access this key right from the right-hand side of your dashboard or from an individual feed page.
HeaderKey.name: X-AIO-Key

QueryKey.description: The AIO Key is used to restrict or grant access to your data. The key is unique, and you can generate a key per feed, and control it in many different ways. The easiest process is to just use your automatically generated master key. You can access this key right from the right-hand side of your dashboard or from an individual feed page.
QueryKey.name: X-AIO-Key

HeaderSignature.description: The AIO Signature is an AWS inspired request signature.
HeaderSignature.name: X-AIO-Signature
